2605.21256.title: Reliable Automated Triage in Spanish Clinical Notes: A Hybrid Framework for Risk-Aware HIV Suspicion Identification
2605.21256.paper_summary: Standard clinical Natural Language Processing (NLP) benchmarks often yield inflated metrics by forcing deterministic classification on ambiguous instances, thereby obscuring the clinical risks of overconfident predictions. To bridge this gap, we propose a risk-aware hybrid selective classification framework, evaluated on early Human Immunodeficiency Virus suspicion identification in Spanish clinical notes. Our dual-verification approach explicitly decouples aleatoric uncertainty through Mondrian conformal prediction and epistemic uncertainty using a Multi-Centroid Mahalanobis Distance veto. Empirical evaluations reveal that standard uncertainty metrics and baseline classifiers are structurally insufficient for safe medical triage, suffering severe coverage collapse when forced to operate under strict reliability constraints. In contrast, by demanding that clinical narratives pass both probabilistic and geometric safeguards, the proposed framework successfully isolates a highly trustworthy operational domain.
2605.21256.paper_summary_zh: 標準臨床自然語言處理（NLP）基準常常通過對模糊實例強制進行確定性分類來產生膨脹的指標，從而掩蓋了過度自信預測的臨床風險。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了一種風險感知的混合選擇性分類框架，並在西班牙臨床筆記中的早期人類免疫缺陷病毒懷疑識別上進行評估。我們的雙重驗證方法通過蒙德里安保形預測明確地解耦了隨機不確定性，並使用多中心馬哈拉諾比斯距離否決來解耦認知不確定性。實證評估顯示，標準不確定性指標和基線分類器在安全醫療分診方面結構上是不足的，當被迫在嚴格的可靠性約束下運行時，遭受了嚴重的覆蓋崩潰。相比之下，通過要求臨床敘事同時通過概率和幾何保障，所提出的框架成功地隔離了一個高度可信的操作領域。
2605.21256.author: Rodrigo Morales-Sánchez et.al.
2605.21256.authors: Rodrigo Morales-Sánchez, Soto Montalvo, Raquel Martínez
